value.found_quote: MCP servers can be configured at three scopes. The scope you choose controls which projects the server loads in and whether the configuration is shared with your team... | Scope | Loads in | Shared with team | Stored in | | [Local](#local-scope) | Current project only | No | `~/.claude.json` | | [Project](#project-scope) | Current project only | Yes, via version control | `.mcp.json` in project root | | [User](#user-scope) | All your projects | No | `~/.claude.json` |
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes MCP Installation Scopes with a dedicated "MCP installation scopes" section. It confirms that MCP servers can be configured at three scopes: local (default, current project only, private, stored in ~/.claude.json), project (current project only, shared via version control in .mcp.json), and user (all projects, private, stored in ~/.claude.json). This directly substantiates the claim that MCP servers can be configured at local, project, or user scope, controlling sharing and loading across configuration files.
